title: Reincarnation & the Life Contract
summary: The soul's cycle and its binding terms — with a named case: Amelia Earhart, reborn.
markdown:
**Reincarnation** and the **life contract** are the archive's account of the soul's journey —
a cycle of incarnations, each governed by planned terms — and the sessions enforce the
doctrine with unusual strictness. The July 2020 material warns against "violating the life
contract" of one's present incarnation; the [Earth Planetary Council](/lore/earth-planetary-council/)
applies the same law even to a stranded [time-traveler](/lore/ori/), denying her extraction
because an incarnation, once begun, must be lived out.

The archive supplies a named case study. Asked about **Amelia Earhart** in the
[Bermuda Triangle](/lore/bermuda-triangle/) session, Unimetrix 1 traces her: the aviator's
craft collided with an extraterrestrial vehicle and fell into a parallel reality, where she
did not survive — and her lineage initiated the "incarnation status protocol." The verdict:
her consciousness lives now as a **22-year-old woman named Sarah Parker in Oklahoma**,
"energetic signature… verified." It is the sessions' most concrete demonstration of the
machinery they describe — death as transit, identity as a thread the record can follow.

The doctrine folds into the larger system: lives stored in the
[Akashic record](/lore/akashic-record/), contracts written against
[parallel timelines](/lore/parallel-timelines/), and mastery of the cycle — choosing one's
returns — listed among the powers of the [far future](/lore/the-chronicle/), where
reincarnation becomes a means of travel rather than a wheel of forgetting.
